generatedSentence: Elara, a seasoned artistic scientist with streaks of gray in her hair, dedicated her life to understanding the unseen forces that plagued her world. She knew a new, virulent virus was slowly spreading, a pathogen with incredible potency that could turn even the most immaculate urban centers into derelict ruins. Her small, opulent garden was her only sanctuary, a place where she could temporarily forget the looming lockdown and the racket of the desperate, halfhearted attempts to contain it. She often felt like she was trapped in a nowhere land, haunted by fuzzy memories of a time before the sickness. Her work, a complex formula she hoped would inoculate humanity, was her primary focus, her personal nirvana. The few guides left reported strange, naughty digital specters—not actual kids, but algorithmic trackers of the contagion—making the entire situation feel even more surreal. The world outside, a trashy landscape of despair, felt like a towering monolith of challenge, an asterisk on humanity's future, a true digital monster. The pressure mounted as her research hit a wall. Elara felt jolted awake by nightmares of a world completely gone, her personal financial stability eroding with every passing day. The public, often engaging in pointless bicker, was growing feral, demanding answers, their meager wages a stark contrast to the fortunes being made by those profiting from the crisis. A rival corporation, led by a cynical moderator, seemed to piggyback on her initial findings, attempting to hijack her discoveries, the truth often veiled behind corporate secrecy. It was a vicious multiplayer game, where all parties were opposed, and the stakes were her very sanity. Her aging knees ached from long hours spent hunched over monitors, her body often nauseous and sickened by the constant stress, feeling truly halfhearted in her efforts at times. She needed a breakthrough, a newer version of her cure, something to energize her dwindling resolve. Her last hope lay in a rare, glowing garnet mineral, rumored to contain properties that could enhance her serum. It was a perilous journey to acquire it, through a city of mazes and faraway dangers, where the threat of residual radiation lingered. Guided by whispers from an ancient dolmen, Elara located the hidden emerald mine, its entrance a perfect hexagon in the rocky facade. Inside, a chaotic kaleidoscope of glowing crystals pulsed with raw energy, almost as if magnetised. Suddenly, a renegade racer, a grizzled adult former ranger whose eyes held a dark venom, appeared, a blur of motion wielding a wicked shuriken, intent on taking the precious nugget. A tense standoff ensued, their shadows dancing in the cinematic lighting provided by the crystals. Elara, quick and nimble despite her age, dodged his attack, feeling a surge of adrenaline that almost felt joyful. The room began to vibrate, a dynamic resonance from the raw energy, rumbling like distant thunder. Her patience was at its ends, her mind fully waking to the raw danger. She knew she had to act fast, to prevent the energy from being transferred into the wrong hands. Her heart overclocked, she deployed a unique spatula-like device, designed to safely extract the encapsulated crystal. Voila! The ground shook, creating a tornado of dust and falling pebble debris. She had been caught in this situation before, but this time, failure was not an option. She felt almost vindicated in her desperate pursuit. With the crystal secured, Elara returned to her lab, easing the tension in her shoulders. The serum, now infused, began its work, the virulent pathogen slowly being undone. She envisioned a future where people could once again visit a vibrant carnival, enjoy a munchy snack from a newsstand, free from the fear of salmonella or the debilitating effects of the disease. Her efforts would glorify a new era of health. She imagined a world listening to a peaceful ballad, not the constant urgent binary of warnings. Though the fight was far from over, this was a significant victory. She felt a deep sense of accomplishment, her conviction hereby stronger than ever. The image of a majestic nautilus shell, a symbol of perfect proportion, came to mind, a calming thought. She stretched her soaked muscles, her hands moving instinctively on a joystick to finalize the data. This wasn't some fairy tale; it was hard-won progress, the old fears chucked aside. A tall larch tree outside her window, once brittle, seemed to sway gently, reflecting the quiet hope she felt. The urgent mission could finally adjourn. She knew many would be interested in this breakthrough. Her spirit, once like a fragile anemone, now bloomed. She took a moment to savor a crisp dacquoise, her mind already planning the next steps, ready to leave the chaotic outside world behind. Her victory was no jerry-built success; it was a carefully planned triumph, helping humanity build a stronger collagen for its future through a grand interchange of knowledge.
panelSentences: Elara, a seasoned artistic scientist with streaks of gray in her hair, dedicated her life to understanding the unseen forces that plagued her world. She knew a new, virulent virus was slowly spreading, a pathogen with incredible potency that could turn even the most immaculate urban centers into derelict ruins. Her small, opulent garden was her only sanctuary, a place where she could temporarily forget the looming lockdown and the racket of the desperate, halfhearted attempts to contain it. She often felt like she was trapped in a nowhere land, haunted by fuzzy memories of a time before the sickness. Her work, a complex formula she hoped would inoculate humanity, was her primary focus, her personal nirvana. The few guides left reported strange, naughty digital specters—not actual kids, but algorithmic trackers of the contagion—making the entire situation feel even more surreal. The world outside, a trashy landscape of despair, felt like a towering monolith of challenge, an asterisk on humanity's future, a true digital monster.

The pressure mounted as her research hit a wall. Elara felt jolted awake by nightmares of a world completely gone, her personal financial stability eroding with every passing day. The public, often engaging in pointless bicker, was growing feral, demanding answers, their meager wages a stark contrast to the fortunes being made by those profiting from the crisis. A rival corporation, led by a cynical moderator, seemed to piggyback on her initial findings, attempting to hijack her discoveries, the truth often veiled behind corporate secrecy. It was a vicious multiplayer game, where all parties were opposed, and the stakes were her very sanity. Her aging knees ached from long hours spent hunched over monitors, her body often nauseous and sickened by the constant stress, feeling truly halfhearted in her efforts at times. She needed a breakthrough, a newer version of her cure, something to energize her dwindling resolve. Her last hope lay in a rare, glowing garnet mineral, rumored to contain properties that could enhance her serum. It was a perilous journey to acquire it, through a city of mazes and faraway dangers, where the threat of residual radiation lingered.

Guided by whispers from an ancient dolmen, Elara located the hidden emerald mine, its entrance a perfect hexagon in the rocky facade. Inside, a chaotic kaleidoscope of glowing crystals pulsed with raw energy, almost as if magnetised. Suddenly, a renegade racer, a grizzled adult former ranger whose eyes held a dark venom, appeared, a blur of motion wielding a wicked shuriken, intent on taking the precious nugget. A tense standoff ensued, their shadows dancing in the cinematic lighting provided by the crystals. Elara, quick and nimble despite her age, dodged his attack, feeling a surge of adrenaline that almost felt joyful. The room began to vibrate, a dynamic resonance from the raw energy, rumbling like distant thunder. Her patience was at its ends, her mind fully waking to the raw danger. She knew she had to act fast, to prevent the energy from being transferred into the wrong hands. Her heart overclocked, she deployed a unique spatula-like device, designed to safely extract the encapsulated crystal. Voila! The ground shook, creating a tornado of dust and falling pebble debris. She had been caught in this situation before, but this time, failure was not an option. She felt almost vindicated in her desperate pursuit.

With the crystal secured, Elara returned to her lab, easing the tension in her shoulders. The serum, now infused, began its work, the virulent pathogen slowly being undone. She envisioned a future where people could once again visit a vibrant carnival, enjoy a munchy snack from a newsstand, free from the fear of salmonella or the debilitating effects of the disease. Her efforts would glorify a new era of health. She imagined a world listening to a peaceful ballad, not the constant urgent binary of warnings. Though the fight was far from over, this was a significant victory. She felt a deep sense of accomplishment, her conviction hereby stronger than ever. The image of a majestic nautilus shell, a symbol of perfect proportion, came to mind, a calming thought. She stretched her soaked muscles, her hands moving instinctively on a joystick to finalize the data. This wasn't some fairy tale; it was hard-won progress, the old fears chucked aside. A tall larch tree outside her window, once brittle, seemed to sway gently, reflecting the quiet hope she felt. The urgent mission could finally adjourn. She knew many would be interested in this breakthrough. Her spirit, once like a fragile anemone, now bloomed. She took a moment to savor a crisp dacquoise, her mind already planning the next steps, ready to leave the chaotic outside world behind. Her victory was no jerry-built success; it was a carefully planned triumph, helping humanity build a stronger collagen for its future through a grand interchange of knowledge.

Her heart **overclocked**, she deployed a unique **spatula**-like device, designed to safely extract the **encapsulated** crystal. **Voila**! With the crystal secured, Elara returned to her lab, **easing** the tension in her shoulders. The serum, now infused, began its work, the **virulent** pathogen slowly being **undone**. Her efforts would **glorify** a new era of health. She imagined a world listening to a peaceful **ballad**, not the constant urgent **binary** of warnings. imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, cyberpunk-infused sci-fi comic book style, thick white gutters, clean black borders, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. Protagonist: A mature woman with gray streaks in her dark, tied-back hair, wearing a practical lab coat over a sturdy jumpsuit, determined expression, visible wrinkles around her eyes. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Elara hunched over a glowing holographic display showing viral structures, surrounded by scientific equipment. Overhead shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Elara navigating through a derelict, mazelike urban street, her face grim, avoiding falling debris, with a silhouette of a rival figure in the distance. Eye-level shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elara, mid-dodge, deflecting a shuriken thrown by a muscular, bearded male 'racer' in protective gear, inside a cave filled with glowing emerald and garnet crystals, dynamic pose. Low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elara, looking weary but satisfied, holding a small, glowing crystal, standing next to a sophisticated bioreactor showing a stabilized viral sample, a quiet sense of triumph on her face. Medium close-up.

definitions.definition: Having or revealing creative skill.
Relating to or characteristic of art or artists.
Aesthetically pleasing.
Artistically, in an artistic style.
A submicroscopic, non-cellular structure consisting of a core of DNA or RNA surrounded by a protein coat, that requires a living host cell to replicate, and often causes disease in the host organism.
A disease caused by these organisms.
definitions.example: He caught a virus and had to stay home from school.

definitions.definition: Venom, as produced by a poisonous animal etc.

A type of malware which can covertly transmit itself between computers via networks (especially the Internet) or removable storage such as disks, often causing damage to systems and data; also computer virus.
Any type of malware.
(of a disease or disease-causing agent) Highly infectious, malignant or deadly.
Hostile to the point of being venomous; intensely acrimonious.
definitions.example: The politicians were virulent in their hatred of the president.

definitions.definition: Lo, there it is; see here; ta-da; presto; behold!
Like trash; containing much trash
definitions.example: a trashy novel

definitions.definition: Having a sound like white noise
definitions.example: trashy cymbals

definitions.definition: A large, single block of stone which is a natural feature; or a block of stone or other similar material used in architecture and sculpture, especially one carved into a monument in ancient times.
Anything massive, uniform, and unmovable, especially a towering and impersonal cultural, political, or social organization or structure.
A substrate having many tiny channels that is cast as a single piece, which is used as a stationary phase for chromatography, as a catalytic surface, etc.
A dead tree whose height and size have been reduced by breaking off or cutting its branches.
To create (something) as, or convert (one or more things) into, a monolith.
A terrifying and dangerous creature.
A bizarre or whimsical creature.
definitions.example: The children decided Grover was a cuddly monster.

definitions.definition: A cruel or antisocial person, especially a criminal.
definitions.example: Get away from those children, you meatheaded monster!

definitions.definition: A horribly deformed person.
A badly behaved child, a brat.
definitions.example: Sit still, you little monster!

definitions.definition: Something unusually large.
definitions.example: Have you seen those powerlifters on TV? They're monsters.

definitions.definition: A prodigy; someone very talented in a specific domain.
definitions.example: That dude playing guitar is a monster.

definitions.definition: A non-player character that player(s) fight against in role-playing games.
To make into a monster; to categorise as a monster; to demonise.
To behave as a monster to; to terrorise.
To harass.
Very large; worthy of a monster.
definitions.example: He has a monster appetite.

definitions.definition: Great; very good; excellent.
(often in the plural) A very small piece of plush material.
definitions.example: You've got a fuzzy on your coat.

definitions.definition: Something covered with fuzz or hair, as an animal or plush toy.
A person, especially a college student, interested in humanities or social sciences, as opposed to one interested in mathematics, science, or engineering.
A soldier with the rank of private.
Covered with fuzz or a large number of tiny loose fibres like a carpet or many stuffed animals
Vague or imprecise.
definitions.example: My recollection of that event is fuzzy.

definitions.definition: Not clear; unfocused.
definitions.example: I finally threw out a large stack of fuzzy photos.

definitions.definition: Any mathematical rule expressed symbolically.
A symbolic expression of the structure of a compound.
definitions.example: H2O is the formula for water.

definitions.definition: A plan or method for dealing with a problem or for achieving a result.
definitions.example: The company's winning formula includes excellent service and quality products.

definitions.definition: A formulation; a prescription; a mixture or solution made in a prescribed manner; the identity and quantities of ingredients of such a mixture.
definitions.example: The formula of the rocket fuel has not been revealed.

definitions.definition: A formal statement of doctrine, as in religion.

A syntactic expression of a proposition, built up from quantifiers, logical connectives, variables, relation and operation symbols, and, depending on the type of logic, possibly other operators such as modal, temporal, deontic or epistemic ones.
A manufactured food simulating human breast milk, designed to be fed to babies and infants under 12 months of age.
Someone who takes part in a race.
A racehorse.
An animal known for its fast speed, or suitable for racing; applied especially to a number of North American snakes, certain kinds of lake trout, etc.
A vehicle or other device used for racing.
Any of several genera of colubrid snakes.
A video game involving racing of any kind.
To invigorate; to make energetic.
To supply with energy, especially electricity; to turn on power to (something).
definitions.example: Whenever we energize that circuit we blow a fuse.

definitions.definition: To use strength in action; to act or operate with force or vigor; to act in producing an effect.
A hard transparent mineral that is often used as gemstones and abrasives.
A dark red color, like that of the gemstone.
Of a dark red colour.
No particular place, noplace.
definitions.example: They went on a cruise to nowhere.

definitions.definition: Unimportant; unworthy of notice.
In no place.
definitions.example: Nowhere did the rules say anything about popcorn.

definitions.definition: To no place.
definitions.example: We sat in traffic, going nowhere.

definitions.definition: Mischievous; tending to misbehave or act badly (especially of a child).
definitions.example: Some naughty boys at school hid the teacher's lesson notes.

definitions.definition: Sexually provocative; now in weakened sense, risqué, cheeky.
definitions.example: I bought some naughty lingerie for my honeymoon.

definitions.definition: Evil, wicked, morally reprehensible.
Bad, worthless, substandard.
A young goat.
definitions.example: He treated the oxen like they didn't exist, but he treated the goat kid like a puppy.

definitions.definition: Of a female goat, the state of being pregnant: in kid.
Kidskin.
The meat of a young goat.
A young antelope.
A child (usually), teenager, or young adult; a juvenile.
definitions.example: She's a kid. It's normal for her to have imaginary friends.

definitions.definition: A person whose childhood took place in a particular time period or area.
definitions.example: He's been living in Los Angeles for years now, but he's a Florida kid.

definitions.definition: One's son or daughter, regardless of age.
definitions.example: He was their youngest kid.

definitions.definition: (in the vocative) Used as a form of address for a child, teenager or young adult
definitions.example: No, kid, you didn't do anything wrong; they did!

definitions.definition: An inexperienced person or one in a junior position.
A deception; an act of kidding somebody.
A small wooden mess tub in which sailors received their food.
To make a fool of (someone).
To dupe or deceive (someone).
To make a joke with (someone).
Of a goat, to give birth to kids.
To joke.
definitions.example: Only kidding

definitions.definition: A fagot; a bundle of heath and furze.
A dart or throwing blade, sometimes with multiple points, used as a weapon by ninja (or samurai).
The condition of being sane.
Reasonable and rational behaviour.
(often in plural) An amount of money paid to a worker for a specified quantity of work, usually calculated on an hourly basis and expressed in an amount of money per hour.
definitions.example: Before her promotion, her wages were 20% less.

definitions.definition: To wager, bet.
To expose oneself to, as a risk; to incur, as a danger; to venture; to hazard.
To employ for wages; to hire.
To conduct or carry out (a war or other contest).
To adventure, or lay out, for hire or reward; to hire out.
To give security for the performance of.
The symbol *.
Something in the shape of or resembling the asterisk symbol.
A blemish in an otherwise outstanding achievement.
definitions.example: They came into the tournament highly ranked, but with a little bit of an asterisk as their last two wins had been unconvincing.

definitions.definition: To mark or replace with an asterisk symbol (*); star.
Something hung up or spread out to hide or protect the face, or hide an object from view; usually of gauze, crepe, or similar diaphanous material.
A cover; disguise; a mask; a pretense.
The calyptra of mosses.
A membrane connecting the margin of the pileus of a mushroom with the stalk; a velum.
A covering for a person or thing; as, a caul (especially over the head)
definitions.example: a nun's veil

definitions.definition: Velum (A circular membrane round the cap of medusa)
A thin layer of tissue which is attached to or covers a mushroom.
An obscuration of the clearness of the tones in pronunciation.
To dress in, or decorate with, a veil.
To conceal as with a veil.
definitions.example: The forest fire was veiled by smoke, but I could hear it clearly.

definitions.definition: Agent noun of track; one who, or that which, tracks or pursues, as a man or dog that follows game.
In an organ, a light strip of wood connecting (in path) a key and a pallet, to communicate motion by pulling.
A type of computer software for composing music by aligning samples on parallel timelines.
A musician who writes music in a tracker.
A computer program that monitors something.
A tracker mortgage.
A snack.
A skirmish; an encounter.
A fight with stones between two parties of boys.
A wrangle; also, a noise, as in angry contention.
The process by which selective eating clubs at Princeton University choose new members.
To quarrel in a tiresome, insulting manner.
definitions.example: They bickered about dinner every evening.

definitions.definition: To brawl or move tremulously, quiver, shimmer (of a water stream, light, flame, etc.)
(of rain) To patter.
To skirmish; to exchange blows; to fight.
A domesticated animal that has returned to the wild; an animal, particularly a domesticated animal, living independently of humans.
A contemptible young person, a lout, a person who behaves wildly.
A person who has isolated themselves from the outside world; one living an alternative lifestyle.

(furry subculture) A character in furry art or literature which has the physical characteristics (body) of a regular animal (typically quadripedal), that may or may not be able to communicate with humans or anthros (contrasts anthro)
definitions.example: The story is about a group of ferals which have to explore the ruins of society after the humans die out.

definitions.definition: Wild, untamed, especially of domesticated animals having returned to the wild.
(of a person) Contemptible, unruly, misbehaved.
Any of various green gemstones, especially a green transparent form of beryl, highly valued as a precious stone.
Emerald green, a colour.
Any hummingbird in the genera Chlorostilbon and Elvira; and some in the genus Amazilia
A size of type between nonpareil and minion, standardized as 6½-point.
To ornament with, or as if with, emeralds; to make green.
Of a rich green colour.
To introduce an antigenic substance or vaccine into something (e.g. the body) or someone, such as to produce immunity to a specific disease.
(by extension) To safeguard or protect something as if by inoculation.
To add one substance to another; to spike.
definitions.example: The culture medium was inoculated with selenium to investigate the rate of uptake.

definitions.definition: To graft by inserting buds.
definitions.example: to inoculate a tree

definitions.definition: To introduce into the mind (used especially of harmful ideas or principles); to imbue; to implant.
definitions.example: to inoculate someone with treason or infidelity

definitions.definition: Complete cessation of suffering; a blissful state attained through realization of sunyata; enlightened experience.
(non-Buddhist) State of paradise; heightened or great pleasure.
Recently made, or created.
definitions.example: This is a new scratch on my car!   The band just released a new album.

definitions.definition: Additional; recently discovered.
definitions.example: We turned up some new evidence from the old files.

definitions.definition: Current or later, as opposed to former.
definitions.example: My new car is much better than my previous one, even though it is older.   We had been in our new house for five years by then.

definitions.definition: Used to distinguish something established more recently, named after something or some place previously existing.
definitions.example: New Bond Street is an extension of Bond Street.

definitions.definition: In original condition; pristine; not previously worn or used.
definitions.example: Are you going to buy a new car or a second-hand one?

definitions.definition: Refreshed, reinvigorated, reformed.
definitions.example: That shirt is dirty. Go and put on a new one.   I feel like a new person after a good night's sleep.   After the accident, I saw the world with new eyes.

definitions.definition: Newborn.
definitions.example: My sister has a new baby, and our mother is excited to finally have a grandchild.

definitions.definition: Of recent origin; having taken place recently.
definitions.example: I can't see you for a while; the pain is still too new.   Did you see the new King Lear at the theatre?

definitions.definition: Strange, unfamiliar or not previously known.
definitions.example: The idea was new to me.   I need to meet new people.

definitions.definition: Recently arrived or appeared.
definitions.example: Have you met the new guy in town?   He is the new kid at school.

definitions.definition: Inexperienced or unaccustomed at some task.
definitions.example: Don't worry that you're new at this job; you'll get better with time.   I'm new at this business.

definitions.definition: (of a period of time) Next; about to begin or recently begun.
definitions.example: We expect to grow at 10% annually in the new decade.

definitions.definition: Not seen or discovered; invisible.
Unskilled; inexperienced.
Not hitherto noticed; unobserved.
Strength
Power
The ability or capacity to perform something.
Luxuriant, and ostentatiously magnificent.
Rich, sumptuous and extravagant.
An outdoor area containing one or more types of plants, usually plants grown for food or ornamental purposes.
definitions.example: a vegetable garden  a flower garden

definitions.definition: The grounds at the front or back of a house.
definitions.example: This house has a swimming pool, a tent, a swing set and a fountain in the garden.  We were drinking lemonade and playing croquet in the garden.  Our garden is overgrown with weeds.

definitions.definition: The twentieth Lenormand card.
A cluster; a bunch.
Pubic hair or the genitalia it masks.
definitions.example: N.B. From a commentary on Song of Solomon 4:16, which was written in Hebrew c. 950 BC; book footnotes are shown here within brackets. Many scholars disagree with this Biblical interpretation, which is included as evidence of the word's usage in 1995 rather than its intended meaning in 950 BC.

definitions.definition: To grow plants in a garden; to create or maintain a garden.
definitions.example: I love to garden — this year I'm going to plant some daffodils.

definitions.definition: Of a batsman, to inspect and tap the pitch lightly with the bat so as to smooth out small rough patches and irregularities.
Common, ordinary, domesticated.
Someone who guides, especially someone hired to show people around a place or an institution and offer information and explanation.
definitions.example: The guide led us around the museum and explained the exhibits.

definitions.definition: A document or book that offers information or instruction; guidebook.
A sign that guides people; guidepost.
Any marking or object that catches the eye to provide quick reference.
A device that guides part of a machine, or guides motion or action.
A spirit believed to speak through a medium.
A member of a group marching in formation who sets the pattern of movement or alignment for the rest.
To serve as a guide for someone or something; to lead or direct in a way; to conduct in a course or path.
To steer or navigate, especially a ship or as a pilot.
To exert control or influence over someone or something.
To supervise the education or training of someone.

Adept at taking or grasping

definitions.definition: Quick-witted and alert.

definitions.definition: In humans, the joint or the region of the joint in the middle part of the leg between the thigh and the shank.

To kneel to.

To move on the knees; to use the knees to move.
To clear of an accusation, suspicion or criticism.

definitions.definition: To lay claim to; to assert a right to; to claim.
To liberate; to set free; to deliver.
To avenge; to punish

definitions.definition: Justified, avenged or cleared of blame
A poison carried by an animal, usually injected into an enemy or prey by biting or stinging.
Feeling or speech marked by spite or malice; vitriol.
To infect with venom; to envenom; to poison.
Poisonous, poisoned; (figuratively) pernicious.
To move or pass from one place, person or thing to another.

To attempt to stop the progression of; to resist or antagonize by physical means, or by arguments, etc.; to contend against.

Acting in opposition; opposing.
Being, of having an opponent; not unopposed.
Opposite.
Property abandoned by its former owner, especially a ship abandoned at sea.
An abandoned or forsaken person; an outcast.
A homeless and/or jobless person; a person who is (perceived as) negligent in their personal affairs and hygiene. (This sense is a modern development of the preceding sense.)
Abandoned, forsaken; given up by the natural owner or guardian; (of a ship) abandoned at sea, dilapidated, neglected; (of a spacecraft) abandoned in outer space.

definitions.definition: Negligent in performing a duty.
Lost; adrift; hence, wanting; careless; neglectful; unfaithful.

Disgusted or revolted.

To run a processor (CPU), or any electronic logic device, at a speed higher than is recommended by the manufacturer.

A purpose, goal, or aim.
One of the two parts of the ground used as a descriptive name for half of the ground.

(in the plural) Money.

Money.

To be excited or roused up; to be stirred up from a dormant, torpid, or inactive state; to be active.
To lay out a body prior to burial in order to allow family and friends to pay their last respects.
To watch, or sit up with, at night, as a dead body.
To be or remain awake; not to sleep.

The act of becoming awake from sleep, or a period of time spent awake.

To become magnetic.

The part of something that faces out; the outer surface.

definitions.definition: The external appearance of someone or something.

definitions.definition: The space beyond some limit or boundary.

definitions.definition: The furthest limit, as to number, quantity, extent, etc.

definitions.definition: A passenger riding on the outside of a coach or carriage.
Of or pertaining to the outer surface, limit or boundary.

Away from the interior or center of something.
Originating from, arranged by, or being someone outside an organization, group, etc.

definitions.definition: Extending or going beyond the borders or scope of an organization, group, etc.
(of a pitch) Away (far) from the batter as it crosses home plate.

definitions.definition: Reaching the extreme or farthest limit, as to extent, quantity, etc; maximum.

To or in the outdoors or outside; to or in an area that is beyond the scope, limits or borders of a given place.

definitions.definition: On the outside of, not inside (something, such as a building).
Beyond the scope, limits or borders of.

definitions.definition: Near, but not in.
(usually with “of”) Except, apart from.

An executable computer file.
A satellite system consisting of two stars or other bodies orbiting each other.
Being in a state of one of two mutually exclusive conditions such as on or off, true or false, molten or frozen, presence or absence of a signal.

definitions.definition: Concerning logic whose subject matter concerns binary states.
Concerning numbers and calculations using the binary number system.
Having two equally important parts; related to something with two parts.

definitions.definition: (computer engineering) Of an operation, function, procedure or logic gate, taking exactly two operands, arguments, parameters or inputs; having domain of dimension 2.

definitions.definition: Of data, consisting coded values (e.g. machine code) not interpretable as plain or ASCII text (e.g. source code).

definitions.definition: Focusing on two mutually exclusive conditions.

A small droplet of water intentionally sprayed on the ice that cause irregularities on the surface.
Transparent and colourless rock crystal.
definitions.example: Brazilian pebble

definitions.definition: A form of slow-burning gunpowder in large cubical grains.
To pave with pebbles.
To deposit water droplets on the ice.
definitions.example: to pebble the ice between games

definitions.definition: To give (leather) a rough appearance with small rounded prominences.
To place a pebble at (a vertex of a graph) according to certain rules; see pebble game.
A specific form or variation of something.
A translation from one language to another.
definitions.example: It's only in the King James Version of the Bible.

definitions.definition: A school exercise, generally of composition in a foreign language.
The act of translating, or rendering, from one language into another language.
An account or description from a particular point of view, especially as contrasted with another account.
definitions.example: He gave another version of the affair.

definitions.definition: A particular revision (of software, firmware, CPU, etc.).
definitions.example: Upgrade to the latest version for new features and bug fixes.

definitions.definition: A condition of the uterus in which its axis is deflected from its normal position without being bent upon itself. See anteversion and retroversion.
An eye movement involving both eyes moving synchronously and symmetrically in the same direction.
A change of form, direction, etc.; transformation; conversion.
definitions.example: External cephalic version is a process by which a breech baby can sometimes be turned from buttocks or foot first to head first.

definitions.definition: To keep track of (a file, document, etc.) in a versioning system.

The confinement of people in their own rooms (e.g. in a school) or cells (in a prison), or to their own homes or areas (e.g. in the case of a city- or nation-wide issue) as a security measure after or amid a disturbance or pandemic, etc.
A contrivance to fasten logs together in rafting.
Someone who moderates
The person who presides over a synod of a Presbyterian Church
A substance (often water or graphite) used to decrease the speed of fast neutrons in a nuclear reactor and hence increase likelihood of fission
A device used to deaden some of the noise from a firearm, although not to the same extent as a suppressor or silencer.
An examiner at Oxford and Cambridge universities.
At the University of Dublin, either the first (senior) or second (junior) in rank in an examination for the degree of Bachelor of Arts.
Someone who supervises and monitors the setting and marking of examinations by different people to ensure consistency of standards.
A mechanical arrangement for regulating motion in a machine, or producing equality of effect.
A kind of lamp in which the flow of the oil to the wick is regulated.
A characteristic or manner of an interaction; a behavior.
definitions.example: Watch the dynamic between the husband and wife when they disagree.

definitions.definition: A moving force.
definitions.example: The study of fluid dynamics quantifies turbulent and laminar flows.

definitions.definition: The varying loudness or volume of a song or the markings that indicate the loudness.
definitions.example: If you pay attention to the dynamics as you play, it's a very moving piece.

definitions.definition: A symbol in a musical score that indicates the desired level of volume.
(grammar) A verb that indicates continued or progressive action on the part of the subject.
Changing; active; in motion.
definitions.example: The environment is dynamic, changing with the years and the seasons.

definitions.definition: Powerful; energetic.
definitions.example: He was a dynamic and engaging speaker.

definitions.definition: Able to change and adapt.
Having to do with the volume of sound.
definitions.example: The dynamic marking in bar 40 is forte.

definitions.definition: Happening at runtime instead of being predetermined at compile time.
definitions.example: dynamic IP addresses

definitions.definition: Pertaining to dynamics, the branch of mechanics concerned with the effects of forces on the motion of objects.
(grammar) Of a verb: not stative, but fientive; indicating continued or progressive action on the part of the subject.
(heading) To capture, overtake.
(heading) To seize hold of.
(heading) To intercept.
(heading) To receive (by being in the way).
(heading) To take in with one's senses or intellect.
(heading) To seize attention, interest.
(heading) To obtain or experience
Of the method of being out in which the striker hits the ball and a fielder catches it.
To lift, raise up.
To augment or make something greater.
To improve something by adding features.
To be raised up; to grow larger.
definitions.example: A debt enhances rapidly by compound interest.

definitions.definition: (radiology) To take up contrast agent (for an organ, tissue, or lesion).
A small, compact chunk or clump.
definitions.example: a gold nugget

definitions.definition: A chicken nugget.
A tidbit of something valuable.
definitions.example: a nugget of wisdom

definitions.definition: A small piece of tasty food, a tidbit.
A type of boot polish.
A bud from the Cannabis sativa plant, especially one that is potent.
An inexperienced, newly trained fighter pilot.
A partial description gleaned from data mining.
Not done.
A chamber pot.
Near this place; nearby.
By this means, action or process.
definitions.example: I hereby declare you husband and wife.

definitions.definition: To exalt, or give glory or praise to (something or someone).
To make (something) appear to be more glorious than it is; regard something or someone as excellent baselessly.
To worship or extol.
To engage the attention of; to awaken interest in; to excite emotion or passion in, in behalf of a person or thing.
definitions.example: Action films don't really interest me.

definitions.definition: To be concerned with or engaged in; to affect; to concern; to excite.
To cause or permit to share.
Having or showing interest.
definitions.example: I'm very interested in going to see that play.

definitions.definition: Motivated by considerations of self-interest; self-serving.
Owning a share of a company.
